reviews.title: Horrific experience
reviews.content: Any type of question you have that isn’t some basic math question instantly breaks the AI. It also has such poor capabilities in understanding basic communication. You just asked it something ? Yea it’s gonna talk about something else that is slightly related, and then proceed to try to ask you a follow up about the off topic discussion. Will also refuse to do any type of research unless you call it lazy, would rather the user “Tell me the right answer so I make sure I’m not giving you the wrong information.” Yes! You heard me! The AI asks the USER for the answer instead of doing research!

reviews.title: urgent bug
reviews.content:
I hope the developers can see this and investigate the issue further. It may be worth considering an urgent update that first checks whether the duplicate Keychain item already exists and safely removes or replaces it before attempting to add a new one.

Same issue here on iOS. Reinstalling, rebooting, disabling iCloud Keychain, disabling iCloud backup for Claude, updating to the latest Claude iOS version did not fix it.

I captured iPhone Console logs. The app itself launches and network/TLS works. Some startup requests return 200, but a specific startup/auth/device request repeatedly fails during launch. Around the same launch, securityd also logs:

Code=-25299 duplicate item
Q6L2SF6YDW.com.anthropic.claude.shared

So this does not look like a simple outage or network issue. It looks like the iOS app has a stale/invalid mobile device auth/session/Keychain state and fails to recover from it. The app should probably update/delete the existing Keychain item instead of calling add and fa
